programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: When a downtrodden janitor, Winston Gooze, is exposed to a catastrophic toxic accident, he’s transformed into a new kind of hero: The Toxic Avenger. Now, Toxie must rise from outcast to savior, taking on ruthless corporate overlords and corrupt forces who threaten his son, his friends, and his community.

programme.title: The Wolf Man
programme.desc: Upon his return to his father's estate, aristocrat Larry Talbot meets a beautiful woman, attends a mystical carnival and uncovers a horrifying curse.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche's father, Big Daddy, comes to Miami for a visit, with an announcement, he's sold the family plantation and is setting out to become a country singer. Meanwhile, Sophia places a curse on a neighbor who refuses to remove his tree that fell over onto the lanai during a storm.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Blanche turns to her daughter Rebecca when her latest beau wants Blanche to bear his children.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred's toothache becomes an exercise in patience for Lamont when Fred refuses to see a dentist and instead tries Bubba's home remedy and a hypnotist. Finally, Fred decides to throw in the towel and visits a dentist but insists on a white dentist.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike continues to make decisions without consulting Gloria.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: When Archie finds out that one of Mike's students is babysitting Joey and she invited her hippie boyfriend over to listen to music, he takes over and brings Joey to his house, even though he is hosting a poker game with his buddies.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: It looks like Archie has turned over a new leaf when he befriends a Jewish man.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Kathleen & Mary vs. Holly & Dianna vs. Donna & Kathy

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Ann & Sheila vs. Pat & Kathy vs. Mardine & Pam

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are bumbling electricians who decide to go away for a rest after they are fired for their incompetence. The rest home they choose is run by Dr. Mallard, a quack who gyps the patients for everything they've got. When the boys discover the crooked goings on they escape, but not before Curly accidentally cures another patient who rewards him with a thousand dollars.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges inherit a women's boutique and fashion clothing for their swanky customers.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are government agent entrusted with protecting professor Snead, who has invented a super rocket fuel. Larry is mistaken for the professor by foreign agents who kidnap the trio and take them to the country of Anemia where they are ordered to produce the rocket fuel or be executed. The boys come up with a concoction they try to pass of as the real stuff, but are exposed when the real professor and his daughter are also kidnapped. The stooges help them escape, using their secret formula to fuel a jeep.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges make a whole batch of homemade beer, but get tossed in jail when Curly sells some to a cop. Their minor indiscretion turns into a forty year sentence when a keg of beer Curly has hidden under his coat explodes while the boys are being photographed. In prison the stooges get into more trouble with the warden and wind on the rockpile when they try to escape. Released as old men with long gray beards, the first thing Curly wants is a bottle of beer.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Trying to patent a new fly-catching device, the Stooges calculate that they need to catch 100,000 flies to pay for it, but nearby crooks overhear their conversation. Unfortunately, they misunderstand the conversation, thinking that the Stoogies won $100,000, and chase the Stooges to get the winnings.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges do a good turn and help a stranger open a safe in what they think is the man's house. Actually the man is a crook and the boys were unwitting accomplices to a robbery. Once they realize what's happened, the stooges go after the bad guy and who's left on the train to Los Vegas. The boys trap the villain and recover the booty.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A professor bets one of his colleagues that he can turn the stooges into gentlemen within 60 days. With the aid of his pretty daughter, the professor tries to teach the boys proper etiquette. After many frustrating attempts, he introduces the stooges into society at a fancy party. At first things go all right, but the party soon degenerates into a wild pie fight.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: During World War I, a German U-boat sinks a British ship and takes the survivors on board. After it takes a wrong turn, the submarine takes them to the unknown land of Caprona, where they find dinosaurs and neanderthals.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: David's parents are moving to Chicago, but David doesn't want to join them. Darlene asks if David can move in with the Conners, but Roseanne and Dan declare it out of the question. Roseanne later learns that David is planning to just run away, so she meets with his mother who comes off as a mean and abusive drunk. She treats David so poorly that Roseanne changes her mind and allows David to move into the Conner house.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: It was Twenty Years Ago Today
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan plan a romantic dinner date to celebrate their twentieth wedding anniversary, but struggle with what to give one another. Dan finds out that Roseanne has decided on something expensive for him, so in order to get Roseanne a nice gift too, he takes a temporary job at Maury's Big and Tall store. His gift idea (and the job to pay for it), which seemed like a good idea at the time, soon blows up in his face.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene wants nothing to do with David after Molly kisses him; and a new friend puts a smile on Bev's face.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene springs a prom-night surprise on David: she has rented a motel room. Meanwhile, Dan is reluctant to share with Roseanne his plans for a new business.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan fear a financial crisis when they cannot sell the house they refurbished and their new partner, Roger, skips town.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan realizes that he needs a steady job to take care of his family when he accidentally knocks out one of Roseanne's teeth on the day she is to meet with a health inspector (who turns out to be Leon); Darlene is accepted to art school but David is rejected.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne worries that Darlene may be planning to run away from home; new neighbor George befriends D.J.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is suffering from empty-nest syndrome as Darlene heads for school. But after Darlene gets settled into her dorm, the two have a revealing mother-daughter chat, and Roseanne returns home with a plan.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Sales are down, again, and the management conclude that they must let one of the staff members go. Also, they decide to leave it up to the staff to decide who should go. Facing the difficult decision, the staff convene at the store on the weekend and battle it out.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: There's a new perfume display at Grace Brothers called 'Bliss' but there's no 'Bliss Girl' to man the display. With no other alternative, Mr. Humphries is placed in charge of the display. And to top it off Captain Peacock has replaced Mr.Humphries. It's going to be a chaotic day on the Ladies and Gents floor.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Toony hosts a Friendsgiving Party, but it doesn't go as planned.
Featured cartoons: Holiday for Drumsticks (1949) Tom Turkey and his Harmonica Humdingers (1940) The Mouse on 57th Street (1961) When the Cat's Away (1935) A Sheep in the Deep (1962)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An old lady who writes popular mystery novels strongly suspects that her niece's husband murdered her, so she exacts revenge by asphyxiating him in her house safe while devising a solid alibi. It's up to Columbo to unravel the subterfuge.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Tired of living with her mother and stepfather and wanting a father figure for Phillip, Carol decides to propose marriage to a man she doesn't love. Meanwhile, married life doesn't seem to be all what it's cracked up to be for Maude and Walter who's argument leads to destruction in the kitchen.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: While waiting for the electoral results for Election 1972, Maude and Walter recall Election 1968. Carol had just gotten a divorce and moved in with Maude. Meanwhile, Walter and Maude's relationship had reached a pivotal moment; should they marry or live together.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: One night after watching Psycho, the girls find it hard to sleep so they stay up and reminisce about how they all came to live with one another.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche is mortified after taking a pregnancy test which indicates she's pregnant. Then after visiting her doctor, she learns the truth, she's beginning menopause. Meanwhile, Dorothy, Rose and Sophia go into the mink breeding business.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The house is fumigated and the girls move into a Miami Beach hotel. Previously, Blanche had won three tickets to see the opening of Burt Reynolds new movie and passes to the party held afterwards where they could meet him. However, on the evening of the opening, the hotel is raided and, mistaken as prostitutes, Dorothy, Blanche and Rose are dragged off to jail.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Bobby "The Brain" Heenan joins Gorilla Monsoon to host another exciting edition of Prime Time Wrestling. In action, Mr. Perfect battles "Rugged" Ronnie Garvin in singles competition, Tito Santana faces "Iron" Mike Sharpe, and more. Plus, highlights from WrestleMania V.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: A new valley girl, Tammy, is like a totally bad influence on Tina; when thwarted, Tammy blackmails Tina with her own erotic friend fiction. Gene and Louise get really angry... nobody but them has the right to blackmail their sister! Bob is hands over heels for "Cake." It's like, tubular! He and Mr. Frond embarrass the kids at school while Tammy makes a noisy exit.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray hires his overwhelmingly annoying cousin Gerard to assist him, and Debra points out how alike the two men are, Ray is mortified. The whiny, nasal voice, the need to find something negative to say about everything, the stupid questions, you could be speaking about either Ray or Gerard.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is bewildered, but not altogether displeased when Debra starts initiating sex on a more than regular basis. He can't believe his luck, he is even willing to give up his golf game to satiate Debra's newfound desires. But he can't leave well enough alone, and decides to look into the reason behind his wife's sudden burst of energy.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug shows his romantic side as Carrie thinks he has lost it; Arthur serves on a jury.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug sees someone driving an R.V. and decides he wants to buy one for himself and Carrie's vacation, however Carrie is planning a trip to Paris for their vacation. So in deciding where to go, they flip a coin.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Immediately after proposing, George tries to postpone his marriage to Susan. Elaine becomes increasingly jealous of George's engagement and has a discussion with her neighbor, a rabbi, who then proceeds to tell the whole neighborhood. Kramer sneaks a caffé latte in the movie theater and burns himself, and he hires Jackie Chiles in an effort to sue the coffee shop.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A maestro impresses Elaine, jeopardizes Kramer's lawsuit and makes Jerry curious about housing in Italy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie's house buzzes with activity as Roseanne tries to hide David from Dan.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne becomes involved in a family feud as Fred sues Jackie for custody of their unborn child; Mark and D.J. tease David for being unmanly.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael arranges a morale-boosting birthday party for Meredith and is determined to come up with the funniest message in her card. Dwight wants a pact with Jim to fight the downsizing, and Michael regrets showing off when Oscar asks for sponsorship for a charity walk being undertaken by his nephew, who has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: The office staff play a basketball game against the warehouse workers in which Jim shows off to impress Pam and upstage her fiancee who is on the opposing team.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Inspired by a tango film, Lisa signs up for dance lessons but the teacher, ex-child star Vicki Valentine, specializes in tap, at which Ralph is surprisingly good but Lisa hopeless. Professor Frink gives her a pair of self-dancing shoes but they go out of control and Lisa has to be tripped up by Homer to disable them. Bart and Milhouse, meanwhile, go camping in the shopping mall, thwarting Wiggum's attempts to flush them out with a puma.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad Marge
programme.desc: Otto's girlfriend seems to be usurping Marge's role when she moves in with the family after a dispute over heavy metal.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A parody documentary in the style of the show 'Behind the Music' uses clips from previous episodes to show how the Simpsons first became famous and the problems that the fame brought them, culminating in an acrimonious break-up. However they all get back together again to appear in a supposed awards show hosted by Willie Nelson but specifically designed to engineer their reunion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: This year's Halloween story sees The Simpsons as "The Munsters" (with everyone except Lisa getting killed), Homer as a wandering spirit on the hunt for a good deed in "G-G-Ghost D-D-Dad," Bart and Lisa as peasants in a fairy tale forest in "Scary Tales Can Come True," and Lisa inadvertently dooming mankind by rescuing a dolphin in "Night of the Dolphin."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While calling Animal Control over a badger taking residence in Santa's Little Helper's doghouse, Homer discovers that Springfield has two different area codes–and ends up leading a revolt that splits the town in two.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: It's Bud's 18th birthday, but everyone still treats him like a little boy. Does he stay at home for his birthday party with a pony and a clown or does Al have something else in mind for his "adult" son?

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Steve Rhoades returns to reclaim his wife, but is shocked to find out that Marcy remarried. The Bundys argue whether friendship is more important than money when they find out that Steve is on the run from the police for stealing a rare egg from a national park.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud wins a ""Dinner with Anthrax"" contest and sends away Al and Peg on a timeshare vacation to Florida while Anthrax visit the house.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Jefferson lets Al in on his psychic phone scam but the Bundys soon take over the business.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud is suspicious when Kelly is invited to a party for smart people.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud juggles two women at once, one being Miss McGowen, his English teacher. Meanwhile, the family ignores Peggy when she is sick and Al gets free pizza by attending children's birthday parties at Chuck E. Cheese.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When Kelly's modeling school suddenly closes down, she gets a new job as a greeter at the TV World theme park. When this is threatened by her low performance ratings, Kelly gets switched into Commercial Land where she plays "the Verminator" who hunts down roaches, like Bud. Meanwhile, Al takes his annual "Bundy World Tour,": a week-long trip around the world by himself, in the Bundy living room.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: In another get rich quick scheme, Fred decides to go down to an escort service with a friend of his and try to apply as escorts. Flatly rejected, Fred decides to open his own escort service complete with false advertising which results in Fred having to escort three ladies in one night.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: The Engagement Man Always Rings Twice
programme.desc: Lamont has been seeing Janet for some time now and he has decided to pop the question. Lamont tells Fred of his plans and he is happy for him. Unfortunately, Fred also discovers after the wedding that the couple does not want to live with Fred.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont has gotten a part in a community play and has great news, George Foreman has agreed to appear in it as well. Dying to meet the boxer, Fred intrudes on the rehearsals and drives the director up the wall, which leads him to quit. This is when Fred steps in as director and makes some immediate changes.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is being honored with the Watts Businessman-of-the-Year Award and he's all hyped up about accepting it. However, an unexpected turn of events lands Fred with a serious back problem and takes up means to try to get rid of it, including, acupuncture, meditation and a corset.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: While Fred is babysitting his soon-to-be grandson, Roger helps a customer and sells him some junk including three medals. After Fred learns of Roger's sale he envisions a story about just how he got those medals. Fred's story has him as a Patton like character fighting during World War II and battling espionage.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Exposure to toxic waste, found under Gus' house, turns Chris into a genius and with Gus he proceeds to enter and win spelling bees.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After eating bad shellfish, Sharon and Gus get amnesia, and Chris seizes the opportunity to convince them that they are his best friends.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: The Dover Boys at Pimento University or The Rivals of Roquefort Hall
programme.desc: A Gay 90's melodrama depicting the exploits of the Dover Boys, Tom, Dick and Larry (of Pimento University), and their rescue of dainty Dora Standpipe (of Miss Cheddar's Female Academy) from the clutches of coward, bully, cad and thief Dan Backslide.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Babbit and Catstello, take-offs on Bud Abbott and Lou Costello try to catch the little Tweety bird, using everything from stilts to dynamite. Trouble is, the tiny bird has a vicious streak in him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A dim-witted, lonesome dog falls in love with a greyhound statue.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: Coal Black and de Sebben Dwarfs
programme.desc: Parody on the Grimm brothers fairy-tale; where So White flees from the Wicked Queen and enlists in the U.S. army as a squad cook.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs is fed up with Cecil winning all the races they compete in. So, Bugs decides to cheat with having different gadgets and costumes on, making Cecil have to finish last for once (maybe).

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: To Duck or Not to Duck
programme.desc: Daffy challenges duck hunter Elmer to a boxing match, rigged in his favor with the collusion of the duck referee. In the stands, Elmer's dog Larrimore suspects that something funny is going on, but he's drowned out by Daffy's all-duck cheering section.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Special carrots make Bugs into a superhero. The first villain he goes up against is Cottontail Smith who is a mad and evil Texas rabbit. Unfortunately, little by little, Bugs' powers start to wear off.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck is an agent representing Sleepy LaGoon, and attempts to sell his talent to Porky Pig.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a women's rights meeting and try to hit on some militant feminists.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After watching infomercials, Beavis and Butt-head invent the ""Butt Scratcher 2000""and try to sell it.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head can't seem to get up and off the couch, but this time it's because they keep drifting in and out of dreamland.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: An oil spill draws hot hippies to the shoreline.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys visit an impotence specialist to find out why they're not scoring.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get locked out of their house and destroy it trying to get back in.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After being threatened at school, Beavis and Butt-head wear disguises to class.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis follows the advice of a motivational speaker and "Seizes the Power."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head can't handle the truth! After witnessing a brutal beating that leaves one man out cold, will the boys lie under oath to protect their idol Todd? If they screw up, there's a serious butt-kicking on the docket, Todd-style.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: While eating a chili dog, Beavis sniffs a piece of onion and begins to tear up, but Butt-Head mistakes it for crying and mocks Beavis relentlessly.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee and Cowboy Curtis take a leap into Magic Screen where they enjoy some mighty fine handcart ridin' until a train chases them back into the waiting arms of Chairry! Then, Cowboy Curtis sings a song with "more" of the secret word in it than you could scream at. Finally, Cowboy Curtis and Miss Yvonne go shopping at Pee-wee's "department store" where they decide to buy Chairry!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Today the secret word is "out," but Pee-wee is stuck in when he overdoes it and comes down with a cold! It's lovely Nurse Yvonne and ultra-health conscious Ricardo to the rescue with some good advice and chicken soup for their sick and cranky friend. In no time, Pee-wee's back to his old self again to join in the Playhouse fun.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max lands tickets to a music awards show in Los Angeles, she and Caroline meet rapper 2 Chainz during the cross-country flight on a private jet.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max pay a steep price for trying to remove a street performer from the entry of their shop.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: On this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host another extraordinary lineup of in-ring action, including Bad News Brown going one-on-one with Hercules on the show's featured battle. Plus, a look at "Macho Man" Randy Savage's appearance on The Brother Love Show, The Twin Towers in action, and more.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As ancient grudges resurface, Rhaenys suggests that Alicent may see reason while the men around her seek bloodshed. Ser Criston proposes a bold scheme. Daemon arrives at Harrenhal to raise an army for the Blacks.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Robert has been injured while hunting and is dying. Jon and the others finally take their vows to the Night's Watch. A man, sent by Robert, is captured for trying to poison Daenerys. Furious, Drogo vows to attack the Seven Kingdoms.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Eddard and his men are betrayed and captured by the Lannisters. When word reaches Robb, he plans to go to war to rescue them. The White Walkers attack The Wall. Tyrion returns to his father with some new friends.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Foreman bonds with the patient of the week, a 16-year-old Romani boy admitted after suddenly becoming unable to breathe during a make-out session with his girlfriend. Meanwhile, House loses his parking space to a new research doctor, Dr. Whitner, and makes a wager with Cuddy to get it back.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day, and Cuddy has a blind date. Meanwhile, House leaves ER duty to take the case of a child patient named Hannah.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A hardworking new employee at the power plant is consumed with resentment at Homer's undeserved success.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Troy McClure presents three Simpsons spinoff spoofs: In "Chief Wiggum, P.I.," Chief Wiggum becomes a New Orleans detective. Next, Grampa Simpson's soul is trapped inside Moe's Love tester machine in "The Love-Matic Grampa." Finally, the Simpson family hosts a '70s-style variety show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Secret War of Lisa Simpson
programme.desc: Lisa joins the all-male military school where Bart is sent for discipline. Then comes the hazing.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson
programme.desc: Designated driver Barney absconds to Manhattan in Homer's car and leaves it there. But when the family journeys to the Big Apple to retrieve their property, Homer discovers his car has been booted.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Unmasked as an impostor, Principal Skinner flees to Capital City to reclaim his former life as a shiftless good-for-nothing. The people of Springfield gain a newfound appreciation for him and form a plan to bring him back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Lisa’s saxophone is destroyed, the Simpsons reminisce about how she originally got the instrument. On the hottest day of the year, Homer must decide whether to buy an air conditioner or replace Lisa’s sax.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In “The Homega Man,” Homer faces a post-apocalyptic Springfield. Next, Bart attempts to become Superfly in "Fly vs. Fly." In “Easy-Bake Coven,” amidst a witch hunt in 1649, Marge and her sisters invent trick-or-treating.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Alarmed by a soccer riot, Homer buys a handgun. His reckless behavior drives Marge and the kids out of the house, and even his new gun club buddies desert him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer becomes the coach of a peewee football team, he replaces star quarterback Nelson with the less talented Bart. Facing his teammates’ scorn, Bart looks for a way out but finds a better way to show his team spirit.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Trying to escape his arranged marriage, Apu asks Marge to pose as his wife during his mother’s visit. When the ruse fails, Homer makes a last-ditch attempt to intervene, but Apu is smitten with his new bride Manjula.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: On a special Raw Saturday Night, Mr. McMahon vows to end "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's reign as WWE Champion by announcing a master plan. The Rock tries to salvage the remains of his Nation by teaming with Mark Henry to take on the New Age Outlaws, X-Pac challenges D-Lo Brown for his European Championship. Plus, Ken Shamrock, Val Venis, and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go to an estate sale. Mutt surprises Alexis with a bike, while Roland asks David to help him shop for a special item.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny talks himself into an unlikely business partnership with Bob. David gets an interview at a woman's clothing store, while Moira reluctantly cares for an ailing Alexis.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline shares her riches-to-rags life story on stage at a storytelling night and captures the attention of a Hollywood studio executive. Meanwhile, Sophie and Oleg search for a surrogate.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max tags along with Caroline as she travels to Los Angeles on a film studio’s dime to discuss the idea of making her riches-to-rags life story into a movie. While there, Max loses her luggage but finds a new crush.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline continues meeting with studio writers in an effort to make her life story into a movie, but she is torn when they reveal they would not portray Max in the project. Also, Max is being spoiled by her new Hollywood beau, Randy, who sets Caroline up on a date with his friend, Bob.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Max pet-sits Randy’s dog, Bruno, and Caroline sets up meetings with actresses who are interested in playing her in the potential biopic, Bruno escapes while a cannibalistic serial killer is loose in their neighborhood.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls’ Hollywood adventure continues as Caroline signs away the rights to her life story so it can be turned a movie. Also, Max is heartbroken when Randy breaks up with her via his therapist, Elliot, and Caroline rents a party bus in an attempt to cheer her up.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Show and Don’t Tell
programme.desc: When the girls go to prison to see Caroline’s father, Martin, perform in a musical, they tell him their plan to use Caroline’s movie money to convert their cupcake window into a dessert bar.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the girls decide to look for a larger space for their dessert bar, Han sets them up with Evie, a real estate agent who has a crush on him. Also, Max is on the fence when Randy surprises her at the diner hoping to patch up their relationship, so she tells Caroline to decide for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Attack of the Killer Apartment
programme.desc: In an attempt to get to know Max’s world better, her boyfriend Randy asks to spend a night at her apartment. Also, Caroline works on getting a liquor license for their dessert bar.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Partnership Hits The Fan
programme.desc: When a law firm courting Randy for a job invites Randy and Max to dinner at a hip new restaurant, the evening veers off course when their eclectic cuisine doesn’t sit well with Max.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline need more space for their renovated dessert bar, they’re willing to do just about anything to convince Angie, the brash owner of the pizza place next door, to rent them her back office.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline discover that Han is in hot water after amassing substantial debt that he cannot repay from gambling on women’s tennis, the diner gang rallies to help him.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Two Openings: Part One & Part Two
programme.desc: As Max and Caroline, now part owners of the diner, put the finishing touches on their newly converted Dessert Bar, Max deals with the aftermath of her recent breakup with Randy, and Sophie and Oleg prepare for the birth of their baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s plans to attract a more sophisticated clientele to their dessert bar take a turn when Max befriends a bouncer whose team of arm wrestlers becomes their latest patrons. Also, Max and Randy continue to text each other, but when she runs out of sexy messages to send him, Oleg offers to be her ghost writer.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg and Sophie’s plan to have Max and Caroline as Barbara’s godparents is derailed when Oleg’s domineering mother, Olga, arrives from the Ukraine for the baby’s baptism and fires Max and Caroline from their godparent duties.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max are invited to speak about their business at Caroline’s alma mater, the University of Pennsylvania, Max takes the opportunity to give Caroline the college partying experience she never had. Also, Oleg’s attempts to bond with baby Barbara are rebuffed by a possessive Sophie, and Han practices his magic tricks with a reluctant Earl as his assistant.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: The newly promoted AFC Richmond face mockery as pundits predict they'll finish dead last this season.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.sub-title: (I Don't Want to Go to) Chelsea
programme.desc: News of a top player looking to move to London sends the English clubs into a frenzy. Richmond play their first game back in the Premier League.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony's power-sharing plan doesn't fly with Johnny Sack, who makes his point through a bookie named Lorraine Calluzzo. Out on parole, Feech La Manna crosses Paulie in his attempt to break into the landscaping business. Tony gets a roommate. Junior looks into the past while testing the patience of those closest to him.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Carmela and Tony reunite, if only to discuss Anthony Jr.'s dwindling college prospects. Feech takes Tony's crew on a trip down memory lane. Lorraine's allegiances get her into hot water. A.J. has a night to remember in the Big Apple.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's grand scheme hits an unexpected snag and forces him to take drastic measures.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: A surprising announcement from one of the humans threatens to dissolve the group.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: The group explores the three main branches of ethical thought.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.sub-title: The Red Dragon and the Gold
programme.desc: In Rhaenyra's absence and with no word from Daemon at Harrenhal, Rhaenys tries to keep the peace on the Black Council as Cole mounts a campaign into the Crownlands. In King's Landing, Aemond continues to undermine Aegon's fragile hold on authority.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball seeks justice when Banana Joe chews Darwin's pen.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin develops a texting addiction when he and Gumball get their first cell phone.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard gets a job as a pizza delivery man, which worries Nicole when strange things begin to occur around the neighborhood.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball, Darwin and Anais go to a haunted-house party.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Anais hunts for hidden treasure after uncovering a secret.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Miss Simian owes Gumball an apology.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin tries to be more direct with Gumball as his teacher.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The guys befriend a compulsive liar.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Bobert becomes Gumball's servant and obeys every command he receives.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Santa Claus is coming to town. Unfortunately he gets run over by the Wattersons, so it's up to them to save Christmas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Two: The Story in Your Eyes
programme.desc: Without Cyborg, Beast Boy feels lost and sets out to find himself, but ends up meeting a music legend.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Three: Playing Hard to Get
programme.desc: The Titans return to the alternate reality to save Cyborg but will they be able to defeat the dragon?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Day the Night Stopped Beginning to Shine and Became Dark Even Though It Was the Day - Chapter Four: The Night Begins to Shine
programme.desc: In a final epic battle, the Titans and their musician friends square off against the dragon for control of the Night Begins to Shine.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin introduces the other Titans to a money-making application but they soon learn the dangers of a shareconomy.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin uses Labor Day as an opportunity to teach the other Titans about the importance of labor.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Control Freak attempts to prove his point about classic superhero cartoons by sending the Titans to a former version of "Teen Titans Go!"

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans build an AI robot so it will create a pizza that's never been made before.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon have a slumber party and watch their favorite TV shows.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon have a slumber party and watch their favorite TV shows.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Justice League's Next Top Talent Idol Star (1)
programme.desc: The Justice League holds a talent competition to find a superhero worthy of joining their team and Robin tries everything to win.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy starts his journey into teenagedom, complete with all of its trials & tribulations.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren thinks TV has ruined Stimpy's mind, but when Stimpy wins a televised Gritty Kitty Litter contest and becomes a Hollywood star on the Muddy Muddskipper show, he leaves Ren behind.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: When Ren falls ill, it's Nurse Stimpy to the rescue, swearing by the Sacred Bedpan to cure his friend. Will the constant care lead to a cured Chihuahua, or a total relapse?

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: During a 36-year mission to the Crab Nebula, Commander Höek succumbs to Space Madness and puts Cadet Stimpy in charge of the mysterious History Eraser Button!

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy need new jobs, so they disguise themselves as Dalmatians, go to work for a psychotic fire chief, and battle a 40-story building fire, saving Mr. Horse and others!

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head miss the schoolbus and decide to take a shortcut home.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head orders a mail-order bride from Russia to ""get some;"" Beavis wants to watch.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Burger World starts a new delivery service; the boys make the first -- and last -- run.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: During wood shop class the boys saw everything they can get their hands on, Beavis then must be rushed to the hospital after an accident happens involving tons of blood, the table saw, and Beavis's finger.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Stewart's family gets a satellite dish; the boys try to enjoy 200 channels of violence despite Stew's mom.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head find a can of root beer and really, really... really shake it up before they open it.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The guys decide to start a band, get a gig, pick a cool name, break up and reminisce about it...all in one day.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys visit an impotence specialist to find out why they're not scoring.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Crazy Crack-Ups (Matt & Cas) vs. Wrecking Crew (Courtney & Kristen)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: Lisa keeps scheming to get Bradfield slimed, but her plans keep backfiring and result in her getting slimed several times.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: The show is back on live after spending the previous week taping a show for network television.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: The network executives have a party on the set and leave money behind. When the cast goes to pick it up, Ross quickly confiscates the cash, claiming it is useless play money. Mr. Dime and Seth Pool get into a battle over the Executive Bathroom, when the lock keeps being changed. Christine, Sarah, Jim, and Mike keep trying to get each other slimed or pied.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Workers at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue, Washington, DC 20001 take on the rest of the world on this week's episode of MXC.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: People Who P!$$ Us Off vs. Worst Jobs
programme.desc: It's unemployment lines and over the liners, as the world's worst jobs take on the people we love to hate. It's the battle of unpleasant work vs. unpleasant jerks. Games - Tumbling Dominoes of Doom, Wallbuggers, Elimination Idol & Surfboard of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Most Extreme Elimination Challenge - Top 25 Most Painful Eliminations of Season 2

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Meat Handlers vs. Cartoon Voice Actors
programme.desc:
The rivalry between the cartoon voice actors and America's meat handlers is finally settled. Plus, Vic gives Kenny advice on being a TV announcer.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Log Drop; Wall Buggers; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Those who take part in twelve step programs challenge those who gave a little bit of themselves in this edition of MXC, and the good captain talks about "tough love".
Games include: Mud Buttlers; Dash to Death; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Brass Balls; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Dairy Workers vs. Automobile Workers (AKA Cows vs. Cars). Who will win out when its lactose versus lube jobs? Games: Wall Bangers, Get Hard!, Saddle Sores, Swish Bucklers, and the Teetering Temple of Crippling Doom.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The good guys challenge the bad guys in this battle. Also, Kenny dresses up as a cop with an attitude and Vic gets into the mind of a criminal.
Games include: Dope on a Rope; Prison Break; Hand Job; Bunk Buddies; Legal Maze; Yankin' It

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Romance is in the air with a very special couples only show. Vic, Kenny, and the Captain discuss the ins and outs of love and relationships.
Games include: Plank Spankers; Romantic Rotating Surfboard of Death; Hot Steaming Bowl of Love; The Sticky Stuff of Love; Brass Balls of Love

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Washed-up child actors go head-to-head with the keen minds of the inventors on this show. Also, overhearing one ex-child actor's catch phrase, Vic and Kenny go crazy-go-nuts.
Games include: Get Over It; Sinkers and Floaters; Door Jam; Le Tour de Grand Prix; Log Drop

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When a history test is stolen, Daria alone can solve the crime - provided she doesn't wake up.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Inspired by a lecture and the tribulations of Thomas Edison and the Wright Brothers, Mr. O'Neill encourages his class to pick something to fail at for personal growth. All his pupils get out of it instead are misery, grief, and despair, including Daria who may be forced to supervise Quinn at an upcoming mall fashion exhibit, and Jane, who's becoming so popular that she might replace Brittany as... a CHEERLEADER!!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While struggling to buy toilet paper for Jake, Daria gets stranded at the Lawndale High homecoming parade, where she meets Jane who plans to meet Tom so they can make fun of everybody. Instead, she runs into Tad Gupty who lost contact with his family(see "Pinch Sitter," Episode #1.8) and then Tom, who she finds is not such a bad guy after all. In fact, he may be more.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Helen has to cancel out of a business junket with Jake, guess who is coerced into taking her place?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffers visit a "spa for the soul" to get their psyches massaged.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A labyrinthine discount-bulk-warehouse superstore serves as the destination for several characters. Jane goes there to buy Gummi Bears (for art supply purposes) after Tom ate hers. Daria accompanies her to buy new shoelaces, because Quinn stole the ones from her boot to repair a bag. Trent and Jesse go there to buy a headlamp and scented candles, respectively, and Tom accompanies them so he can find Jane and make up with her after their argument. (Walking with the two musicians, their half-brained purchase ideas drive him nearly up the wall.) The Fashion Club goes to the warehouse store to buy sunscreen and various other beauty needs, and, of course, their incredibly high standards prevent them from making any actual purchases. The free-sample cheese logs at the store become quite popular-an obsession, in fact, for hungry Mr. DeMartino, whose food budget is incredibly tight, and for Mrs. Johansen (a 300-pound hypoglycemic woman who is a recurring character). Kevin's dad, Doug, is holdi

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Is Lawndale a pretty little suburb, or an unholy nexus of evil haunted by the tortured denizens of the Beyond?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn discovers her spiritual side and we all have to live through it.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The phantom time traveling grandfather clock has reappeared, this time in Windsor castle. When DM and Penfold investigate the scene, they encounter the disgruntled time traveler Master Snozzle, King Arthur's first and former wizard.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Great Britain is in complete chaos as every cup of tea has turned into low grade dishwater. Baron Greenback is collecting the remaining tea supply by tractor ray beam to his teapot shaped satellite, the TB1.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Have You Fled From Any Good Books Lately?
programme.desc: Penfold receives an intergalactic delivery: a free gift from the Cosmos book of the light year club. It's the 'Cowards Pop-up book of Slobbering Monsters by I.L. Scaremstiff. Once he opens it, J.J. Quark unleashes a remote animation ray from space to bring the monsters to life.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K has had word that Greenback knows the location of the long lost amulet of Egganofice, a medallion fashioned by an ancient Egyptian sorcerer in 5002 B.C. which possesses unlimited mysterious powers. In truth, Greenback plans to let Danger Mouse do the hard work for him and grab the amulet once DM finds it for him.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Professor Squackencluck is lost on an island in the China sea and feels the need to be rescued. Dangermouse deduces that the island in question must be Combaktu and sets off on a daring mission, accompanied by Penfold of course. On arrival, a tricky Witch doctor manages to split our heroes up. While DM is matching wits with the doctor, Penfold finds himself in the clutches of an Uran Utang.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Homemade Volcano, Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Walk Through Paper, Heat Spiral and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Homemade Volcano. Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Heat Spiral, Heat From your Body, Paper Fold, Computer Plane and Walk Through Paper.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Bird Flies, Bleach Art, Tricks with Gravity, Fire Extinguisher and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Fish Bombing Run, How a Bird Flies, Impossible Holes, Bleach Art, Woolly Aphids, Tricks with Gravity, Computer Explosions, and Home Made Fire Extinguishers.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Flour, Pendulum, Leaf Color, How a Siren Works, Blow out Candle, Guitar, Aurora, Crush can and more
programme.desc: n this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Flour Explosion, Pendulum, Hidden Leaf Color, How Sirens Work, Mapping a Guitar, Blown Out Candle, Card Illusion, Aurora Borealis, Come Back Can and Crush Gallon Can.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Speed of Sound, Refraction, Trick, Light Bulb Filament, Glass Duet, Spell Check, Stars and more
programme.desc: Speed of Sound, Selective Refraction Trick, How Many Stars, Glass Duet, Spelling Checker, After a Forest Fire, Window Oscillation, Asparagus, Finding a Tree's Height. Mr. Wizard measures the difference between hearing the sound of a starter pistol over the walkie-talkie and hearing it through the air.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The wonders of carbon; Thomas Edison.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The usefulness of gyroscopes; how to keep a healthy heart.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The origins of steel; how photographs are made.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: The sun; how some animals change throughout their lives..

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: How vacuum cleaners work; how cloth is made.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Each planet is different. They are all different sizes – Pluto’s the smallest, and Jupiter’s the biggest. They come in a variety of colors – Mars is covered with rust, so it looks red; the methane (cold natural gas) in the atmosphere of Uranus makes it look blue; and Saturn’s colorful rings are made of icy rock. As far as we know now, Earth is the only planet in our solar system that is home to living things.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: When you push something, you’re using pressure. Pressure depends on two things – the power of the push and the area that’s being pushed on. A push on a small area makes more pressure than the same size push on a big area. Pushing hard on something creates more pressure than a little nudge, naturally.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The guys try to retrieve a family heirloom from an old man.
